introduction: Acute inflammation of the middle ear due to pyogenic organisms.

features: Later, perforation and pulsating mucopurulent discharge

investigations: Ear swab for MCS - swab taken properly without contamination

non_drug: Myringotomy for persistent mucopurulent collection in middle ear with bulging eardrum
drug: Amoxicillin - Adult: 500 mg -1 g orally 8 hourly for 5 - 7 days
Paracetamol - Adult: 500 mg - 1 g orally 4 - 6 hourly (maximum of 4 g/d) for 5 - 7 days
Paracetamol - Child over 50 kg: same as adult dosing
Paracetamol - 3 months - 1 year: 125 - 250 mg for 5 - 7 days
Psuedoephedrine - Adult: 60 mg orally 4 – 6 hourly, (maximum 4 times a day)
Psuedoephedrine - Child 6-12 years: 30 mg (5 mL of syrup) 8 hourly
Psuedoephedrine - Child 2-5 years:15 mg, (2.5 mL of syrup) 4-8 hourly
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Preparations of pseudoephedrine with antihistamines may cause drowsiness

complications: Intracranial (meningitis, brain abscess, lateral sinus thrombosis)
prevention: General healthy and clean airy environment reduces incidence of upper respiratory infections (colds)
